home.heroSubtitle: I'm Eduard, a Software Engineer at Mjølner Informatics in Aarhus. Calm under complexity, meticulous about quality, and motivated by long-term solutions for business-critical systems.
home.aboutP1: I'm a Software Engineer and IT Consultant based in Aarhus, with five-plus years across the public sector, defence, retail, and renewable-energy SaaS. Most recently a multi-system run inside Netcompany across Danish public-sector platforms (KOMBIT VALG elections, STIL / Uddannelse.dk education, and adjacent municipal systems). The throughline is quality-focused delivery: backend and frontend feature work, requirements clarification, automated testing, and the kind of stakeholder coordination that lowers delivery risk. I joined Mjølner Informatics in April 2026 after closing my chapter at Netcompany; before that came Greenbyte, Boozt, and Systematic.
home.aboutP2: Romanian by birth, Danish by residence, European by inclination. I hold an MSc in Engineering: Technology-Based Business Development from Aarhus University (2022 – 2024), and a BSc in Software Technology Engineering from VIA University College in Horsens. The marketing diploma I started with at IBA Kolding still pays off; it left me with a habit of reading a brief carefully before writing a line of code.
home.aboutP3Lead: What I look for in a team: meaningful systems, calm review culture, and colleagues who treat correctness as the long game.
home.aboutP3LinksHint: The /my-story page has the longer arc; the /now page has whatever has my attention this month.

featured.kombit-valg: Full-stack engineering on Denmark's national administrative election platform, C# / .NET, Angular, and the integrations every Danish kommune depends on at poll time.
featured.sitaware: Internship on Systematic's NATO-grade C2 suite. Java + Angular feature work, defect triage, and Robot Framework UI test automation under FDD.
featured.greenbyte-saas: Three years on a renewable-energy SaaS platform. Full-stack .NET Core + React, and architect and lead developer of the Flutter / Dart mobile companion app.
featured.boozt: PHP / Symfony backend on a large-scale Nordic fashion retailer. Feature work, automated testing, and a Kanban flow brought to the team.

blurbs.kombit-valg: Full-stack engineering on Denmark's national administrative election platform. C#/.NET on the back end, Angular on the web.
blurbs.sitaware: Internship on Systematic's NATO-grade command-and-control suite. Java and Angular feature work, plus Robot Framework UI test automation.
blurbs.greenbyte-saas: Three years on a renewable-energy SaaS platform. Full-stack .NET Core and React, plus architect and lead developer of the Flutter mobile companion.
blurbs.boozt: PHP / Symfony backend for one of the larger Nordic fashion retailers. Feature work, automated testing, and a Kanban flow brought to the team.

writing.heading: Notes from practice, and from the master's bench.

personal.heading: Outside the office.
personal.description: Borussia Dortmund on Saturdays, long drives on weekends, and a slow, growing list of countries on a wall map.

sections.footballLede: Following Borussia Dortmund through the Bundesliga calendar.
sections.footballBody: I picked up Dortmund years ago and have followed them since: Westfalenstadion when the schedule and the budget align, the rest from the sofa. A steady kind of support, kept up because the team is mine and because Saturdays are better with a match on.
sections.carsLede: Driving as decompression. Long detours through Jutland and the occasional autobahn run.
sections.travelLede: Cities for the food, mountains for the silence, coastlines for the wind.

bvb.seasonSummary: Borussia Dortmund are competing in the Bundesliga and the DFB-Pokal this season; live standings, fixtures, and results return as soon as the feed is back online.

myStory.heading: How I got here.
myStory.description: A chronological version of the CV: the choices, not the achievements. Dated, honest, and short. The technical detail of each chapter lives on <work>/work</work>; the steadier-life slice lives on <personal>/personal</personal>.
myStory.metaDescription: The longer arc, from AP Marketing in Kolding to engineering in Aarhus. Chronological, dated, calm.
myStory.whatsNextLead: Currently at Mjølner Informatics in Aarhus, working on systems that carry real weight in a calm review culture that takes quality seriously. Pairs with <now>/now</now>, which holds the volatile slice.
